76.reason: // Missing break after insertion; loop continues and increments count again, causing double counting.

68.reason: Missing break statement. After appending a new node at the end of the list, the loop should terminate. Without this break, the loop condition `p != NULL` will eventually fail when `p` becomes `q` (which has `link = NULL`) in the next iteration step `p=p->link`, but it's safer and clearer to break explicitly after insertion. More critically, without the break in the inner `if`, the code falls through to the `else` block in the next iteration or behaves unpredictably. Actually, looking closely: if we insert in the `if(p->link!=NULL)` block, we modify `p->link`. The next `p=p->link` in the for loop update will move to the newly inserted node. Then `p->link` is the old next node. The loop continues. This is logically flawed because we've already placed the word. We must break after insertion. Same for the `else` block (appending to end).

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing break statement. After appending a new node at the end of the list, the loop should terminate. Without this break, the loop condition `p != NULL` will eventually fail when `p` becomes `q` (which has `link = NULL`) in the next iteration step `p=p->link`, but it's safer and clearer to break explicitly after insertion. More critically, without the break in the inner `if`, the code falls through to the `else` block in the next iteration or behaves unpredictably. Actually, looking closely: if we insert in the `if(p->link!=NULL)` block, we modify `p->link`. The next `p=p->link` in the for loop update will move to the newly inserted node. Then `p->link` is the old next node. The loop continues. This is logically flawed because we've already placed the word. We must break after insertion. Same for the `else` block (appending to end).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing break statement. After appending a new node at the end of the list, the loop must terminate to avoid re-processing or accessing invalid memory in subsequent iterations for the same word.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Similarly, missing 'break' after appending to the end. Without it, the loop would have already exited naturally, but in combination with the above missing break, the real issue is that when a match is not found early, multiple insertions can occur during the same traversal. However, the critical flaw is the absence of 'break' after successful insertion in the middle, which causes the loop to keep running and potentially insert the same word again at the tail.]
